idea.hook: Think Voyager 1 is leaving the Solar System fast?
facts: Voyager 1, our fastest outgoing probe, travels at an incredible ~17 km/s (over 38,000 mph).
To truly exit our Solar System (reach the Oort Cloud's outer edge, ~100,000 AU away) would take Voyager 1 over 28,000 years.
At the speed of light, that same Oort Cloud journey takes under 1.6 years. Reaching the nearest star, Proxima Centauri (4.2 light-years), would take Voyager 1 over 75,000 years!
idea.payoff: Interstellar travel at current speeds is a multi-generational quest, making light speed not just a goal, but a necessity for true exploration.
idea.title: Voyager's REAL Galactic Road Trip! 🚀

scenes.visual: Distant shot of Voyager 1 probe leaving Earth's vicinity, small dot against a starry background. A subtle arrow indicates its outward path.
Text appears over a graphic showing a stylized speedometer or speed lines with a small Voyager 1 icon, accelerating rapidly across the screen.

scenes.text: To Oort Cloud edge (~100k AU): 28,000+ years for Voyager 1.
scenes.visual: Infographic showing a vast expanse with a tiny, slow-moving dot representing Voyager 1 and a distant, faded ring representing the Oort Cloud. A digital timer graphic rapidly counts upwards.

scenes.text: At light speed, Oort Cloud: <1.6 years. Nearest star (4.2 LY): 4.2 years.
scenes.visual: A brilliant, glowing beam of light streaks across the screen from left to right, quickly passing distant stars. Text appears quickly, emphasizing speed.

scenes.text: Voyager 1 to Proxima Centauri: 75,000+ years! Light speed is a necessity.
scenes.visual: A dramatic split screen. Left side: Faint, struggling Voyager 1 with a massive, daunting '75,000+ YEARS' ticker. Right side: A brilliant, inviting tunnel of light with 'LIGHT SPEED: A NECESSITY!' text. Both sides converge slightly.
